chunks.text: PHYSICAL REVIEW A VOLUME 52, NUMBER 6 DECEMBER 1995 Phase of the atomic polarization in high-order harmonic generation Maciej Lewenstein, ' Pascal Salieres, ' and Anne L'Huillier' 'Service des Photons, Atomes, et Molecules, Centre d'Etudes de Saclay, 91191Gifsur-Yve-tte, France Department ofPhysics, Lund 1nstitute ofTechnology, S221-00Lund, Sweden (Received 22 June 1995) A recently formulated theory of high-order harmonic generation by low-frequency laser fields [Anne L'Huillier et al. , Phys. Rev. A 48, R3433 (1993)]allows us to study the phase of the induced atomic dipole moment. We show that this phase exhibits a piecewise-linear dependence on the laser intensity. This depen- dence can be interpreted in quasiclassical terms, and is related to the action acquired by the electron during its motion in the laser field. The value ofthe phase, however, is also affected by the quantum effects oftunneling, diffusion, and interference. The phase ofthe dipole moment considerably influences the conversion efficiency, as well as the coherence properties, ofthe harmonics generated in macroscopic media. PACS number(s): 32.80.Rm, 42.65.Ky I.INTRODUCTION field. Obviously, if this phase depends strongly on the laser intensity, it will be different for different atoms in the inter- High-order harmonic generation (HG) in rare gases is one action volume, due to the spatial variations of the intensity. ofthe most rapidly developing topics in the physics ofatoms In the same way, it will be time dependent, owing to the interacting with intense laser pulses [1—3].The theoretical variation of the intensity during the laser pulse. In a recent description of HG requires the solution of the time- Letter [18],we have shown that the phase ofthe dipole mo- dependent Schrodinger equation [4] describing an atom in ment is a piecewise-linear rapidly decreasing function of — the laser field.
